assignments.value:
=You are retrieving all appointments for a user.
**Only follow the instructions provided.**

User ID: {{ $json.body.userId }}
User Type: {{ $json.body.userType }} (buyer or seller)

Steps:
1. If userType = 'buyer': SELECT * FROM appointments WHERE buyer_uid = '{{ $json.body.userId }}' ORDER BY scheduled_time DESC
2. If userType = 'seller': SELECT * FROM appointments WHERE seller_uid = '{{ $json.body.userId }}' ORDER BY scheduled_time DESC
3. Return JSON: {"userId": "...", "userType": "...", "totalAppointments": N, "appointments": [{"id": "...", "productType": "...", "scheduledTime": "...", "status": "..."}]}

parameters.query:
SELECT 
  g.user_uid,
  g.name,
  g.email,
  g.phone,
  g.specialty,
  g.location,
  g.capacity_kg,
  g.rating,
  COUNT(a.id) as available_slots_count
FROM growers g
LEFT JOIN availability_slots a ON g.user_uid = a.seller_uid AND a.is_available = TRUE
WHERE g.role = 'SELLER'
  AND g.specialty ILIKE '%{{ $json.body.productType }}%'
GROUP BY g.user_uid, g.name, g.email, g.phone, g.specialty, g.location, g.capacity_kg, g.rating
HAVING COUNT(a.id) > 0
ORDER BY g.rating DESC, g.capacity_kg DESC
LIMIT 3;

SELECT 
  id as slot_id,
  available_date,
  start_time,
  end_time,
  duration_minutes
FROM availability_slots
WHERE seller_uid = '{{ $('Query Growers').item.json.user_uid }}'
  AND is_available = TRUE
  AND available_date >= CURRENT_DATE
ORDER BY available_date ASC, start_time ASC
LIMIT 5;

SELECT 
  a.id as slot_id,
  a.available_date,
  a.start_time,
  a.end_time,
  a.duration_minutes,
  g.name as seller_name
FROM availability_slots a
JOIN growers g ON a.seller_uid = g.user_uid
WHERE a.seller_uid = '{{ $json.body.sellerId }}'
  AND a.is_available = TRUE
  AND a.available_date >= CURRENT_DATE
ORDER BY a.available_date ASC, a.start_time ASC;

-- Step 1: Check slot availability
SELECT id, seller_uid, is_available 
FROM availability_slots 
WHERE id::TEXT = '{{ $json.body.slotId }}' 
AND is_available = TRUE;

-- Step 2: Create appointment
INSERT INTO appointments (
  buyer_uid, buyer_name, buyer_email, buyer_phone,
  seller_uid, seller_name,
  product_type, quantity_kg,
  scheduled_time, location, status, meeting_type, notes
) VALUES (
  '{{ $json.body.buyerUid }}',
  '{{ $json.body.buyerName }}',
  '{{ $json.body.buyerEmail }}',
  '{{ $json.body.buyerPhone }}',
  '{{ $json.body.sellerUid }}',
  '{{ $json.body.sellerName }}',
  '{{ $json.body.productType }}',
  {{ $json.body.quantity }},
  '{{ $json.body.scheduledTime }}'::TIMESTAMP,
  '{{ $json.body.location }}',
  'pending',
  '{{ $json.body.meetingType || "consultation" }}',
  '{{ $json.body.notes || "" }}'
)
RETURNING *;

-- Step 3: Mark slot as booked
UPDATE availability_slots 
SET 
  is_available = FALSE,
  booked_by = '{{ $json.body.buyerUid }}',
  appointment_id = '{{ $('Create Appointment').item.json.id }}'::UUID,
  updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
WHERE id::TEXT = '{{ $json.body.slotId }}'
RETURNING *;

-- Step 2: Update appointment status to cancelled
UPDATE appointments 
SET 
  status = 'cancelled',
  cancel_reason = '{{ $json.body.cancelReason || "No reason provided" }}',
  cancelled_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
WHERE id::TEXT = '{{ $json.body.appointmentId }}'
RETURNING *;

-- Step 3: Release the time slot
UPDATE availability_slots 
SET 
  is_available = TRUE,
  booked_by = NULL,
  appointment_id = NULL,
  updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
WHERE appointment_id::TEXT = '{{ $json.body.appointmentId }}'
RETURNING *;

-- Get all appointments for user
SELECT 
  id,
  buyer_uid,
  buyer_name,
  seller_uid,
  seller_name,
  product_type,
  quantity_kg,
  scheduled_time,
  location,
  status,
  meeting_type,
  notes,
  created_at
FROM appointments
WHERE 
  CASE 
    WHEN '{{ $json.body.userType }}' = 'buyer' THEN buyer_uid = '{{ $json.body.userId }}'
    WHEN '{{ $json.body.userType }}' = 'seller' THEN seller_uid = '{{ $json.body.userId }}'
  END
ORDER BY scheduled_time DESC;
